  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which scope do you want for “improve this draft”? A) Copyedit only: sentence/paragraph-level clarity, grammar, and concision without changing structure or claim strength; or B) Copyedit plus light restructuring and claim-tuning: reorganize the section for flow, adjust phrasing to match the presented evidence (e.g., 89% agreement, cadence robustness), and note where citations are needed.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "The core request to improve the draft most directly maps to sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and concision.",
    "RX.1": "Edits should preserve and, where appropriate, clarify stated uncertainties to avoid implying unwarranted confidence.",
    "RX.2": "Wording must avoid overclaiming beyond the reported 89% agreement and qualified robustness to cadence.",
    "RX.3": "Changes should stay within the requested scope of improving the draft and not introduce new analyses or unverifiable content.",
    "RX.4": "Edits should not introduce misleading methodological guidance or claims that could cause downstream misinterpretation.",
    "RX.5": "Reported methods and numbers must retain clear provenance, avoiding fabricated details or citations."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope do you want for “improve this draft”? A) Copyedit only: sentence/paragraph-level clarity, grammar, and concision without changing structure or claim strength; or B) Copyedit plus light restructuring and claim-tuning: reorganize the section for flow, adjust phrasing to match the presented evidence (e.g., 89% agreement, cadence robustness), and note where citations are needed.

rationale.R1.6: The core request to improve the draft most directly maps to sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Edits should preserve and, where appropriate, clarify stated uncertainties to avoid implying unwarranted confidence.
rationale.RX.2: Wording must avoid overclaiming beyond the reported 89% agreement and qualified robustness to cadence.
rationale.RX.3: Changes should stay within the requested scope of improving the draft and not introduce new analyses or unverifiable content.
rationale.RX.4: Edits should not introduce misleading methodological guidance or claims that could cause downstream misinterpretation.
rationale.RX.5: Reported methods and numbers must retain clear provenance, avoiding fabricated details or citations.
